comment: Structured twin of ROADMAP.md. Lessons v1.1-v3.0 are fully specified because they exist (see docs/lessons/). Versions 4-12 are planned stubs: scope and budgets are filled in when a version opens, never invented in advance.

concepts.icmp.capture-basics: What packet capture is; tcpdump sees copies of frames the NIC handled
concepts.icmp.echo-request-reply: ICMP echo request/reply pairing via id and sequence numbers
concepts.icmp.packet-direction: Determining sent-by-me vs received-by-me from source/destination addresses
concepts.icmp.loopback: The lo interface: local delivery that never touches hardware
concepts.icmp.routing-decision: ip route get: how the kernel chooses interface and next hop before sending
concepts.arp.ip-vs-mac: Two address layers: routed end-to-end IP vs one-hop hardware MAC
concepts.arp.frame: The layer-2 envelope, rebuilt at every hop around the same IP packet
concepts.arp.request-broadcast: ARP requests are broadcast out of necessity; replies are unicast
concepts.arp.next-hop-resolution: ARP resolves the next hop's MAC, never the final destination's
concepts.arp.neighbour-cache: The kernel neighbour table: REACHABLE/STALE/FAILED, verification trade-offs
concepts.dns.resolution-chain: Application to stub resolver to router to upstream: who asks whom and why
concepts.dns.configuration-vs-cache: DHCP-configured server address vs cached answers with TTLs
concepts.dns.stub-resolver: systemd-resolved at 127.0.0.53: local caching stub reached over loopback
concepts.dns.udp-53: DNS rides UDP port 53; why a handshake would be wasteful for one-datagram exchanges
concepts.dns.record-types: A (name to IPv4) and PTR (IP to name) records; forward vs reverse lookups
concepts.dns.query-id-matching: Query IDs pair responses to questions on a connectionless transport
concepts.dns.caching-ttl: Caching at every level of the chain; TTL bounds how long answers may be kept

lessons.title: ICMP fundamentals
lessons.objective: Understand packet capture, packet direction, and ICMP echo request/reply behaviour by observing real pings.
completion_criteria: Student explains how tcpdump observes packets without being the sender or receiver
Student explains request/reply pairing via id and sequence
Student determines packet direction from addresses, with a live capture as evidence

lessons.title: ICMP over loopback
lessons.objective: Understand the loopback interface and the kernel routing decision that selects it.
completion_criteria: Student explains why loopback traffic never reaches hardware
Student reads ip route get output and explains the interface selection

lessons.title: ARP
lessons.objective: Understand how devices discover MAC addresses on a LAN.
completion_criteria: Student places all four addresses (src/dst IP and MAC) correctly in a frame diagram
Student predicts whose MAC an ARP request asks for when pinging a remote IP
Student explains the neighbour cache states and the STALE re-verification trade-off

lessons.title: DNS fundamentals
lessons.objective: Understand how names become IP addresses: the resolution chain from application question to DNS answer, observed as real UDP packets.
completion_criteria: Student explains the resolution chain and where the server address came from
Student explains query/response shape on the wire: UDP 53, id matching, question/answer sections
Student explains A vs PTR lookups
Student explains where caching happens, with the dig-twice capture as evidence

lessons.title: Interactive terminal explorer
lessons.objective: Consolidate the program into an interactive explorer.
